text: In software development, testing plays a crucial role in ensuring that applications work correctly. There are different types of tests, each with a specific purpose. Unit tests examine individual components, like a single function, in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of the system work together as expected. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before the actual code. This approach forces them to think carefully about what the code should do. Ultimately, a comprehensive testing strategy builds confidence. Knowing that thorough tests pass allows developers to make changes and add new features with less fear of breaking things.
